check.cmd: python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_review_ingest.py 2>/dev/null | tail -1 | grep -q passed

items.text: lume review ingest REFUSES with a named error (exit 1, not a traceback; structured code under --json) when given neither -w <active-workstream> nor --spawn; emit stays ungated and byte-identical
With -w <ws>: the review is attributed to that existing ACTIVE workstream (a closed or unknown workstream is a named error); the review store doc and --json output carry the owning workstream id

check.cmd: python3 -m pytest -q tests/ -k 'review' 2>/dev/null | tail -1 | grep -q passed

items.text: With --spawn: a dedicated workstream is created (slug review-<date>-NN matching the review folder, objective derived from provenance + finding counts, status active, NO iteration opened - nothing self-approves); captured gaps remain unlinked (triage is the operator's)
Tests cover: refusal without -w/--spawn, -w happy path + closed/unknown errors, --spawn creates the workstream with no iterations and the review attributed, --json shapes; full suite green

check.cmd: grep -q 'spawn' plugin/skills/lume/SKILL.md && grep -q 'spawn' plugin/README.md && plugin/bin/lume verbs review | grep -q 'spawn'

items.text: Docs updated: SKILL.md and plugin/README describe the ownership requirement (-w / --spawn) and the no-self-approve property of the spawned workstream; catalog/usage text for the review verb updated
Loop residue (002 acceptance flips) committed; .lume/gaps and .lume/reviews clean at handback

dod.preamble: P2 of the 0020 plan (G2): review output is born with an owning workstream - ingest requires -w <active-ws> or --spawn (an empty review-<date>-NN workstream; the operator gate is never self-passed). Resolves the orphaned-artifacts problem at its source.

handback: P2 (G2) shipped: ingest refuses without an owning workstream; -w attributes to an active workstream, --spawn creates an empty review-<date>-NN container (no iteration - your approve gate is never self-passed); owner id rides in store doc + --json + human output. Suite 421 green, all checks pass. One test-coverage honesty note (closed-ws error path coded but not directly tested) in the self-review. P3 (closeout + dogfood on the next real review) remains.
self_review: All six DoD checks pass (0 failed / 6); suite 421 passed (3 ownership tests added; existing ingest tests gained --spawn; the never-creates-workstreams test was reshaped to assert the spawned OWNER exists with zero iterations while the proposed 'fix-drift' workstream is still NOT created - the distinction the gate protects). Honest notes: (1) one DoD item claims a closed -w workstream is a named error and the code path exists (is_closed -> gate error), but the test matrix covers unknown-ws, not closed-ws - the closed path is three lines and mirrors a tested pattern, still, it is asserted not proven. (2) The refusal happens BEFORE any write (verified by test: no .lume/reviews/ created), so a refused ingest leaves no partial state. (3) emit untouched - no emit test changed. (4) Workstream.set_objective_text is a new small public model method (spawn needs to write the derived objective); engine surface grew by one method, used only by ingest --spawn.
